session_title: Vox Populi? Responding to the Rise of Populism
session_description: A Townhall session at the Munich Security Conference 2026 examining the global rise of populism. The discussion explores the causes of populism across the political spectrum, distinguishes its left and right variants, and asks whether populist elements should be incorporated into mainstream politics or contained. Panelists debate strategies and policy responses, the depth of political reform needed to address public dissatisfaction, how to restore trust in democratic institutions, and how to revive citizens' confidence in their collective ability to shape the future.

roster.name: Katrin Bennhold
roster.title: Host of The World Newsletter (Moderator)
roster.bio: German-British journalist and host of The World newsletter at The New York Times. Former Berlin bureau chief for the NYT, where she covered German politics, European affairs, and the rise of right-wing populism in Europe including the AfD. Earlier in her career she reported on gender, inequality, and labor from Paris and London for the International Herald Tribune. Known for long-form interviews with European political figures and for framing transatlantic political shifts for an American audience.

roster.name: Petr Pavel
roster.title: President of the Czech Republic
roster.bio: President of the Czech Republic since March 2023. Retired Czech Army general and former Chairman of the NATO Military Committee from 2015 to 2018, the first official from a former Warsaw Pact country to hold that position. Ran for president as an independent on a platform of restoring democratic norms, supporting Ukraine, and anchoring Czechia firmly in the EU and NATO, defeating populist former prime minister Andrej Babiš. Frequently speaks about the threat of populism and disinformation to Central European democracies and about the need for strong transatlantic defence cooperation.

roster.name: Manfred Weber
roster.title: President of the European People's Party; Chairman of the EPP Group in the European Parliament
roster.bio: German politician, leader of the European People's Party (EPP) since 2022 and chairman of its parliamentary group in the European Parliament since 2014. Member of the CSU, the Bavarian sister party of Germany's CDU. Was the EPP's lead candidate (Spitzenkandidat) for European Commission President in 2019. Defends the political center-right in Europe against both far-right populists and the far left, and has been outspoken on EU enlargement, migration policy, support for Ukraine, and the question of whether and how mainstream parties should engage with the populist right.

roster.name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
roster.title: U.S. Representative, Member of the House Committee on Energy and Commerce
roster.affiliation: United States House of Representatives, New York's 14th congressional district
roster.bio: American politician serving as U.S. Representative for New York's 14th congressional district since 2019, the youngest woman ever elected to the U.S. Congress. Member of the Democratic Socialists of America and a leading figure of the progressive left in the United States. Known for championing the Green New Deal, Medicare for All, and tax reform targeting wealth concentration. Frames her politics as a response to economic inequality and the failures of neoliberalism, and is often cited in debates about whether the populist label applies meaningfully to left-wing as well as right-wing movements.

roster.name: Daiana Fernández Molero
roster.title: Member of the Commission on Foreign Relations and Worship, Chamber of Deputies
roster.bio: Argentine politician serving in the Chamber of Deputies of the Argentine National Congress, with a seat on the Commission on Foreign Relations and Worship. Alumna of the Munich Security Conference's Women Parliamentarians Program 2024. Speaks from the perspective of a Latin American legislator on questions of populism, having served during a period of dramatic political realignment in Argentina under the Milei presidency. Brings a Global South vantage point on how populist movements interact with economic crisis, institutional fragility, and foreign policy in emerging democracies.
